message:
perf(sanitize): Add fast path for exact matches in isSemanticallyBlocked

The isSemanticallyBlocked function was performing string normalization and
comparison for every blocked entry on every call. This adds a fast path
that checks for exact/near-exact matches using content hashes before
falling back to the more expensive character-by-character comparisons.

Changes:
- Compute content hash (case/whitespace normalized) for the input content
- Build a Set of hashes for all blocked entries
- Return true immediately if the content hash is in the blocked set
- Only proceed to detailed string comparisons if hash lookup misses

The hashContent function already normalizes case and whitespace, making
it suitable for detecting semantically equivalent content that differs
only in formatting. This optimization is particularly effective when:
- The blocked list is large (Set lookup is O(1) vs O(n) iteration)
- The same blocked list is checked repeatedly (hash computation is fast)
- Most checks are against non-blocked content (fast negative path)

New tests verify:
- Exact content matches (with whitespace/case differences) are detected
- Unrelated content correctly returns false
